market_summary: Indian benchmarks snapped a two-day losing streak on Wednesday, 1 July 2026, with the Nifty 50 reclaiming the 24,000 mark. The Sensex rose 443.97 points (+0.58%) to 76,922.64 and the Nifty 50 added 140.85 points (+0.59%) to 24,005.85, led by FMCG, auto, realty and consumer names, while IT was the day's biggest drag. The advance came alongside strong macro data: June GST collections rose 13.9% year-on-year to Rs 1,94,812 crore. On the flows side, provisional data showed foreign institutional investors (FIIs) net sold while domestic institutional investors (DIIs) were heavy net buyers, with DII buying more than absorbing the foreign outflow.
data_confidence_note: Today's official close (Wed 2026-07-01) is cross-verified exactly across two Tier-A sources: the Upstox market-wrap and Business Standard, both reporting Sensex 76,922.64 (+443.97, +0.58%) and Nifty 50 24,005.85 (+140.85, +0.59%). Weekday and date verified (Wednesday, 1 July 2026) and clearly distinct from Tuesday 2026-06-30 (Sensex 76,478.67 / Nifty 23,865.75). The June GST figure (Rs 1,94,812 crore, +13.9% YoY) is Finance Ministry provisional data corroborated by ANI, Business Today and other outlets. Sector moves and FII/DII provisional flows (FIIs -Rs 2,556.75 cr, DIIs +Rs 6,842.34 cr) come from the Upstox market wrap; flows are provisional end-of-day figures and are presented directionally. The exact Bank Nifty closing level could not be cross-verified from a correctly-dated primary source, so no specific Bank Nifty number is featured (no fabricated figures). Reused for the Reels pipeline (verified, sourced, unchanged) — this reel reports on the 2026-07-01 (Wednesday) market session.

top_3_stories.headline: Sensex jumps 444 points and Nifty reclaims 24,000 as the market snaps a two-day slide
top_3_stories.what_happened: Indian benchmark indices ended a two-day losing streak on Wednesday. The Sensex rose 443.97 points (+0.58%) to close at 76,922.64 and the Nifty 50 added 140.85 points (+0.59%) to end at 24,005.85, moving back above the psychologically watched 24,000 level. The rebound was broad, powered by FMCG, auto and realty names, and helped the benchmarks recover the ground lost in the previous two sessions.
top_3_stories.why_it_matters: Reclaiming 24,000 on the Nifty after a two-session decline shows buyers stepped back in at lower levels, with strength spread across domestic-facing sectors rather than a single heavyweight. It marks a shift in short-term tone back toward the upside, even as export-led IT lagged.
top_3_stories.investor_takeaway: For educational context only: a broad-based bounce that reclaims a round-number level like 24,000, led by domestic sectors while IT lags, illustrates rotation and short-term sentiment - not a directional call. Not investment advice.

sources.name: Upstox - Market Wrap, July 1: Nifty50 ends above 24,000, Sensex rises 444 points
Business Standard - Stock Market Close: Sensex gains 444 pts, Nifty ends at 24,006

top_3_stories.headline: June GST collections rise 13.9% year-on-year to Rs 1.94 lakh crore
top_3_stories.what_happened: Provisional Finance Ministry data showed India's gross Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections climbed to Rs 1,94,812 crore in June 2026, a 13.9% increase over Rs 1,71,105 crore in June 2025. The growth was led by import revenue, which surged 34.6% to Rs 60,038 crore, while gross domestic revenue grew a more modest 6.5% to Rs 1,34,774 crore. Net GST revenue for the month stood at Rs 1,62,377 crore, up 11.2% year-on-year.
top_3_stories.why_it_matters: GST collections are a widely watched real-time gauge of economic activity and consumption. A near-14% year-on-year rise points to resilient underlying demand and tax buoyancy, and the surge in import-linked revenue signals firm domestic consumption of imported goods - a supportive macro backdrop for the day's move in consumption-facing sectors.
key_numbers: Gross import revenue: Rs 60,038 crore, +34.6% YoY
Gross domestic revenue: Rs 1,34,774 crore, +6.5% YoY
Net GST revenue: Rs 1,62,377 crore, +11.2% YoY
top_3_stories.investor_takeaway: For educational context only: rising GST collections are one indicator of economic momentum and consumption strength, useful as backdrop rather than as a signal on any stock or index. Not investment advice.

sources.name: ANI - India's gross GST collections surge 13.9% YoY to Rs 1.94 lakh crore in June 2026
Business Today - GST collections for June grow 13.9% to Rs 1,94,812 crore

top_3_stories.headline: Domestic institutions outbuy foreign sellers as DIIs absorb FII outflows
top_3_stories.what_happened: Provisional exchange data for the session showed foreign institutional investors (FIIs) were net sellers of about Rs 2,556.75 crore in the cash market, while domestic institutional investors (DIIs) were net buyers of roughly Rs 6,842.34 crore. Domestic buying was more than double the foreign selling, helping the market rebound even as overseas investors trimmed positions.
top_3_stories.why_it_matters: The FII-sell / DII-buy divergence has been a defining feature of this market. When domestic institutions absorb foreign outflows on this scale, benchmarks can rise despite FII selling - which is precisely what played out on the day. It illustrates the growing role of domestic flows (mutual funds, insurers) as a stabiliser.
key_numbers: DII net buying ~2.7x FII net selling
top_3_stories.investor_takeaway: For educational context only: FII and DII provisional flows describe who was buying and selling on the day and help explain index moves; they are end-of-day estimates, not a recommendation. Not investment advice.

sources.name: Upstox - Market Wrap, July 1, 2026 (FII/DII provisional flows)
